cryptRopen 0.2.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
  • Changelog

Dashboard summary of an asynchronous crypt_r() job

Source: R/cryptR_job.R
summary.cryptR_job.Rd

Companion to print(). Returns a structured list (class summary.cryptR_job) carrying the aggregates a monitoring script typically needs: total task count, per-state counts, elapsed seconds, active workers, total rows processed (sum over resolved tasks — excludes NA), output path, and the full per-task status dataframe.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'cryptR_job'
summary(object, ...)

Arguments

object

A cryptR_job object.

...

Ignored.

Value

An object of class summary.cryptR_job — a named list with elements n_tasks, counts, elapsed_sec, n_workers, total_rows, output_path, log_written, status (the full cryptR_status() dataframe).

Details

The object has its own print() method that renders a compact dashboard.

See also

cryptR_status(), cryptR_collect().

Other async_job: cryptR_collect(), cryptR_results(), cryptR_status(), cryptR_wait(), crypt_r()

Examples

# \donttest{
# Build a tiny job (see ?crypt_r for a more detailed walkthrough)
work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "cryptR_summary_example")
mask_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "mask")
out_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "output")
int_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "intermediate")
for (d in c(mask_dir, out_dir, int_dir)) {
  dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
}

input_file <- system.file("extdata", "persons.csv", package = "cryptRopen")
mask <- data.frame(
  folder_path     = dirname(input_file),
  file            = basename(input_file),
  encrypted_file  = "persons_crypt.csv",
  vars_to_encrypt = "email",
  vars_to_remove  = NA,
  to_encrypt      = "X",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
writexl::write_xlsx(mask, file.path(mask_dir, "mask.xlsx"))

job <- crypt_r(
  mask_folder_path  = mask_dir,
  mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",
  output_path       = out_dir,
  intermediate_path = int_dir,
  encryption_key    = "demo-key",
  n_workers         = 1L
)
job <- cryptR_collect(job)
summary(job)
#> <cryptR_job summary>
#>   tasks        : 1
#>     running    : 0
#>     done       : 1
#>     failed     : 0
#>   workers      : 1
#>   elapsed      : 1.11 s
#>   rows total   : 10
#>   output_path  : /tmp/RtmpoVdRJG/cryptR_summary_example/output
#>   log_written  : TRUE

unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE)
# }
